currentState: Mentor and former ally broken into warning and refusal. Chapter 21 shows Muradin as the last person still trying to speak to Arthas from the world before Frostmourne, and Arthas as already beyond being turned back by that history.
powerDynamic: Muradin holds experience, local truth, and the moral authority of an elder who knows the cost. Arthas holds decision force, then the blade itself. Once Frostmourne is claimed, Muradin can still name the wrongness but can no longer command the outcome.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Muradin is the one who steps first toward Arthas in the cave, trying to close the old mentor distance before the room proves that distance no longer exists.
Their final chapter-21 contact is violent and asymmetrical: Muradin moving in with living-body urgency, Arthas answering through Frostmourne and the breaking chamber.

nameUsage.muradinCallsArthas: Uses Arthas in the plain spoken personal register of someone who knew the prince before the blade.
nameUsage.arthasCallsMuradin: Any response reads as completed severance; history no longer moderates his certainty.

history.event: Muradin briefs Sylvanas and Jaina as a man who still believes the warning should matter if delivered in time.
Inside the cavern Muradin confronts Arthas directly and is wounded when the blade and chamber answer that confrontation with overwhelming force.

trajectory: Campaign comrades and mentor bond -> chapter 21's fatal proof that Frostmourne outranks every older loyalty.
